sections.paragraph:
τρίτος, η, ον (τρεῖς; Hom.+)

① third in a series, third

ⓐ used as adj., w. a noun that can oft. be supplied fr. the context ἕως τρίτου οὐρανοῦ 2 Cor 12:2 (GrBar 10:1; ApcSed 2:4; ApcMos 37.—IdeVuippens, Le Paradis terrestre au troisième ciel 1925. Also EPeterson, TLZ 52, 1927, 78–80. Further lit. s.v. οὐρανός 1e). τὸ τρίτον ζῷον Rv 4:7. Cp. 6:5ab; 8:10a; 11:14. τρίτον γένος PtK 2 p. 15, 8 (s. γένος 3). τρίτου βαθμοῦ GJs 7:3. μίλιον τρ. 17:2. (ἐν) τῇ τρίτῃ ἡμέρᾳ (Appian, Liby. 122 §578; JosAs 29:8; AscIs 3:16; Just., D. 97, 1) Mt 16:21; 17:23; 20:19; Lk 9:22; 24:7, 46; Ac 10:40. τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ τρίτῃ Lk 18:33; J 2:1; 1 Cor 15:4. (SMcCasland, The Scripture Basis of ‘On the Third Day’: JBL 48, 1929, 124–37; GLandes, JBL 86, ’67, 446–50 [Jonah]. See s.v. τρεῖς). ἕως τρίτης ἡμέρας Mt 27:64 (cp. TestAbr A 20 p. 103, 23 [Stone p. 54] ἕως τρίτης ἡμέρας). μετὰ τρίτην ἡμέραν after three days (Appian, Iber. 43 §177) Ac 10:40 D. τρίτη ὥρα (=nine o’clock in the morning) 20:3 or ὥρα τρίτη Mk 15:25 (AMahoney, CBQ 28, ’66, 292–99); Ac 2:15. τρίτη ὥρα τῆς νυκτός (=nine o’clock at night, as TestAbr A 5 p. 82, 11 [Stone p. 12]) Ac 23:23. ἐν τῇ τρ. φυλακῇ Lk 12:38.—τρίτην ταύτην ἡμέραν (Lucian, Dial. Mort. 13, 3; Achilles Tat. 7, 11, 2; s. ἄγω 4) Lk 24:21. ἄλλος ἄγγελος τρίτος Rv 14:9.—The noun is supplied fr. the context (Diog. L. 2, 46 Ἀριστοτέλης ἐν τρίτῳ [i.e., book] περὶ ποιητικῆς) Mt 22:26; Mk 12:21; Lk 20:12, 31; Rv 16:4; 21:19. τῇ τρίτῃ (sc. ἡμέρᾳ. Likew. τῇ τρίτῃ GDI p. 874, n50b, 3 [Chios about 600 b.c.]; Demosth. [I a.d.] in Aëtius 186, 16; Appian, Bell. Civ. 2, 88 §369; Arrian, Anab. 7, 11, 1. Cp. Jos., Vi. 229 εἰς τρίτην) Lk 13:32 (looking toward the fut. after σήμερον and αὔριον=the day after tomorrow; cp. Epict. 4, 10, 31; 4, 12, 21; M. Ant. 4, 47.—Looking back at the past, the third day would = ‘the day before yesterday’. Cp. Ps.-Lucian, Halc c. 3 ἑώρας τρίτην ἡμέραν ὅσος ἦν ὁ χειμών=the day before yesterday you experienced how severe the storm was); Ac 27:19. Sc. ἔτος GJs 7:1.

ⓑ used as adv. τὸ τρίτον the third time (Hom. et al.; PLips 33 II, 15; TestAbr A 13 p. 92, 18 [Stone p. 32]), τρίτον a third time (Aeschyl. et al.; Jos., Ant. 8, 371), both in the sense for the third time Mk 14:41; Lk 23:22; J 21:17ab. In the same sense ἐκ τρίτου (Pla., Tim. 54b; ParJer 7:8; Aelian, VH 14, 46) Mt 26:44. τρίτον τοῦτο now for the third time, this is the third time (Judg 16:15) 2 Cor 12:14; 13:1. τοῦτο ἤδη τρίτον J 21:14 (s. FNeirynck, ETL 64, ’88, 431f). In enumerations (τὸ) τρίτον in the third place 1 Cor 12:28; D 16:6 (cp. Pla., Rep. 2, 358c; Plut., Mor. 459d; Just., A I, 26, 1; Iambl., Vi. Pyth. 29, 165 πρῶτον … δεύτερον … τρίτον; 30, 171).

② as a subst. a third part of someth., third, third part τὸ τρίτον (sc. μέρος; s. B-D-F §241, 7.—τὸ τρ. in this sense Diod S 17, 30, 3; Lucian, Tox. 46 τὸ τρ. τῆς ἀτιμίας; PFlor 4, 17; 19; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 402 I, 18 τὸ νενομισμένον τρίτον=the third in accordance w. the law; Num 15:6, 7) the third part, one-third foll. by partitive gen. (Appian, Illyr. 26 §75 τὸ τρ. τούτων) Rv 8:7–12; 9:15, 18; 12:4.—DELG s.v. τρεῖς. M-M. EDNT. TW. Sv.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

adj. adj. = adjective

oft. oft. = often

  1. fr. = from

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

TLZ TLZ = Theolog. Literaturzeitung—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

τρ.̠tr. = translate, etc.

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

AscIs AscIs = Ascension of Isaiah—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

CBQ CBQ = Catholic Biblical Quarterly—List 6

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Achilles Tat Achilles Tat , IV a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

GDI GDI = Sammlung der griechischen Dialekt-Inschriften—List 3

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

fut. fut. = fut.

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PLips PLips = Griech. Urkunden der Papyrussammlung zu Leipzig—List 4

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

ETL ETL = Ephemerides Theologicae Lovanienses—List 6

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Iambl Iambl , phil., III–IV a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

someth. someth. = something

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

foll. foll. = followed, following

gen. gen. = genitive

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1016
